description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped rock with a weathered and slightly angular form. Its surface is predominantly rough, featuring a mix of muted gray and brown tones with subtle hints of green, likely from moss or lichen. The rock has a natural, unpolished appearance, with visible textural variations and small crevices across its exterior. Its base is relatively flat, suggesting it could rest stably on the ground, while the upper surfaces are uneven and slightly sloped. The coloration and texture indicate it is composed mainly of granite or a similar hard stone, typical of rocks found in landscaped urban environments. There are no visible markings, carvings, or signs of artificial modification on its surface.
description: A medium-sized, rough-textured gray-brown rock with subtle greenish patches and an irregular, angular shape. Its surface is weathered and natural, with no visible markings or modifications.
description_view_0: From this view, the rock appears broad and slightly flattened on top, with a rough, mottled surface and subtle greenish patches.
description_view_1: This side view highlights the rock's sloped profile and uneven base, showing more pronounced surface texture and angularity.
description_view_2: Seen from this angle, the rock looks narrower and more elongated, with a visible ridge running along its length.
description_view_3: This view reveals the rock's widest side, displaying a mix of rough and slightly smoother areas with natural color variations.
attribute_location: urban park or landscaped sidewalk edge
